RS
RP

RS
RP
A
ISSUED WITH TENDER / CLIENT COMMENT

RS
RP

INTRODUCTION
EQUIPMENT SUMMARY
SCOPE OF SUPPLY AND WORK
APPLICABLE DRAWINGS, STANDARDS & SPECIFICATIONS
EXECUTION
SPARES
GUARANTEE
REJECTION
QUALITY REQUIREMENT
VENDOR DATA REQUIREMENT
Page 3 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
This Job Specification along with its attachments broadly describes in detail the scope
of supply & work of the Contractor, suggested execution methodology, applicable
drawings (existing and new) and standards / specifications applicable, vendor data
requirements etc. for the revamp of the existing

text.b: implementation of reliability enhancement recommendations at BPCL Mumbai

page.text: The Contractor's scope includes Project management, execution, residual engineering,
detailing, fabrication drawing preparation, procurement, pre-fabrication / fabrication
(including supply of materials except for Free issue materials as covered in tender),
inspection, assembly, pre-dry-out, site erection and testing (in presence of OEM
representative / supplier for free issue / bought out items), assistance during pre-
commissioning / commissioning & PGTR etc. for the following Fired Heater and APH
System:
ITEM No. DESCRIPTION
TYPE
DUTY
MMKCal/Hr
MOC of Coil
(Revamp)
CDU3 Heater
Existing Multicellular box type
Vertical tube heater with dual
fired burners and having
balanced draft system
(Outboard Cast and glass
type APH with two FD fans
and one ID fan). Shares a
Common structure mounted
stack with the adjoining VDU
heater
(Existing)
(Target duty)
P5

text.b: Time is the essence of this project

page.text: . Revamp of the existing heater as defined in the
subsequent clauses is to be completed in a limited period shut down. Important
contractual dates / schedules related to start / completion of work are available
elsewhere in Commercial section of the tender document
For details of facilities / utilities
if any, (e.g., construction power, water, storage space etc.) contractor to refer
commercial portion of tender.
Contractor shall accordingly consider maximum pre fabrication in modular forms
permitted as per ODC limits and ensure mobilization of resources
i.e., materials in
Contractor’s scope of supply, manpower and tools & tackles.
Also, activities shall be
carried out in parallel to strictly adhere to the shutdown duration.
Open Space for site work shall be provided by Client within refinery premises for storage
and module / assembly purposes. All pre-fabricated items / supplies shall be brought in
refinery premises in weather proof protection packing considering rain etc. Tarpaulin
wrapping shall be done for refractory lined pre dried out duct / modules. Temporary
blinding shall be done for openings to prevent ingress of water etc. Any Hydra, trailer &
crane etc. for movement / erection of modules etc. to heater location shall be in
Contractors scope if not specifically mentioned in commercial section.
Bidders, after studying the contents of this package may decide to obtain clarifications
before making their offer. Any ambiguity shall be discussed at the pre-bid stage and once
Page 4 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
having done so, the offer shall be in strict compliance to the requirements and technical
specifications of this package.
Various technical specifications / drawings (existing and revamp) / documents as
referred to in this specification and the List of Contents form part of this tender. Any
deviation / exception listed in the bid will be disregarded and treated as null and void.
Any deviation, if listed anywhere in the Offer, will lead to technical rejection without any
further reference to bidder.
The offer submitted by the Contractor shall not contain any write-up on scope of supply
/ service, scope of work, documents/drawings considered for bidding etc. which are
already covered in the tender. Any such write-up shall be ignored for the purpose of bid
evaluation.

text.b: Only documents as per Vendor data requirements related to
qualification & Technical compliance are to be submitted. No other technical
details should be submitted with the Offer.
Bidder shall note that as this is a NO DEVIATION tender
details are required to be submitted in the offer and by the act of submission of
bid, it is assumed & implied that Bidder’s offer is in perfect compliance to the
complete bidding document. Bidders should not bid if they have any deviation

page.text: For successful Contractor any deviation at the execution stage, which becomes
unavoidable, shall be immediately submitted for PMC / OWNER approval and Contractor
shall not proceed with such deviation till its acceptance by PMC / OWNER. Time taken /
delay, if any, in processing of deviation permit or its rejection shall be solely to
Contractor’s
account.
It shall be the overall responsibility of the Contractor to carry out residual detailing,
fabricate, assemble, erect and test the equipment in conformity with codes /
specifications and to meet all requirements of package.
In the event of conflict among the various documents attached with the tender document,
following order of precedence shall apply. However, as a general rule, more stringent
requirement shall govern:
(i) Statutory
/ Owner’s
Requirements.
(ii) Heater GADs, Datasheets and P&IDs attached with the tender.
(iii) This specification and other job specifications referred herein.
(iv) EIL standards & Standard specifications.
(v) Codes and Standards.
However, all such conflicts shall be referred to PMC / OWNER for clarification at the
earliest and the decision of PMC / OWNER shall be final and binding on the Contractor
without any cost and delivery implications.
The prospective Contractors shall make a site visit to familiarize themselves with the
following before submitting the bids:
Details of the existing heater system and the space available
Site facilities
Approach roads / routes
Storage facilities
Movement constraints and restrictions
Page 5 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
Area for crane placement / hard stand
To understand and plan the prefabrication, modularization scheme, sequence of
pre fabrication / pre dry out and erection and plan adequate mobilization
To understand and identify erection enabling activities associated with the
execution of work defined in tender document
Local / Owner safety & statutory requirements (in regard to local barricading, local
isolation with safety precautions for hot work in running refinery)
Safety of all personnel and equipment
To plan all other activities that are not highlighted but are essential in order to
execute the total work completion as defined in the tender in the limited shut down
duration.
Contractor is required to execute multiple works parallelly in same area considering short
shut down period. Hence, Contractor to plan execution methodology in consultation with
Owner / PMC.
1.10 To facilitate site erection, the fabrication shall be done in modules & assemblies
prefabricated in the Vendor’s
shop in line with modularization scheme reflected in the
tender drawings / documents, so as to minimize the site work during the shutdown
period. The erection modus operandi to be adopted by the Contractor shall be highlighted
in the offer in detail along with bar charts. All balance activities are to be finished in the
shutdown period specified.
1.11 Contractor shall consider site conditions, approach roads, cable trench, drains, space
availability, utility lines battery limit and all issues enabling the erection of the items
covered in the Scope of work. Clarifications, if any, shall be obtained before submitting
his bids. No such issue will be entertained during the execution stage for compensations.
Contractor shall be asked to give detailed presentation on modular construction /
execution methodology before / after award of job and all Owner / EIL comments shall
be incorporated.
1.12 The following activities are required to be carried out by the Contractor in-house. Sub-
contracting of these activities is not allowed:
Project management of execution of Heater revamp works
Procurement as applicable
Construction supervision at shop / site
Quality assurance
1.13 Aspiring bidder should be an established Contractor and have sufficient resources and
experienced staff to execute the job. In order to technically qualify for this job, the
prospective Bidder should meet the relevant PTR (proven track record) requirements
specified in commercial section of tender. The necessary supporting documents are also
to be submitted.
Before undertaking any activity in the Heater package works, Contractor to ensure that
Contractor or his subcontracting agencies shall meet the experience criteria as specified
in commercial section of tender. Where the Contractor does not meet the experience
criteria w.r.t. a particular activity for which sub-contracting is allowed, he shall
mandatorily employ the services of a suitable sub-Contractor who meets the experience
criteria.
Requirements of sub-contracting shall be strictly complied with. Evaluation of
Contractor
’s / proposed sub
-Contractor
’s
experience for the various activities shall be
Page 6 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
done post award and shall be taken up with successful Contractor during kick-off
meeting. Necessary documentation shall be submitted for approval and Owner / EIL
concurrence obtained before subcontracting.
It shall be mandatory on the part of Contractor to depute such sub-Contractors and key
personnel as listed in their details submitted during their approval by Owner / EIL
1.14 A list of approved suppliers for various major items to be procured by the Contractor is
given in the tender. Contractor is required to adhere to this vendor list only. At the time
of order placement, Contractor shall ensure that the selected Vendor is not on Holiday
List of Owner / EIL and not having any limitation / constraints for the items and the
respective Supplier / Vendor must have Proven Track Record (PTR) of the items as given
below. Contractor shall ensure the compliance to the PTR requirements and obtain EIL
concurrence before Sub-ordering.
In case the Vendor list do not include the list for any item / component to be used in the
Heater Package, the same shall be brought to the notice of Owner / EIL and the selected
Vendor shall meet the PTR requirements as given below:
a. Supplier for expansion joints of fired heater system should have executed at least
2 validly similar jobs (same type, service, metallurgy, size and capacity as
applicable) in past seven years out of which at least one should be in operation
for one year.
b. Supplier for other components (tube support castings, skin thermocouples, ,
refractory items etc.) in heater systems should have executed at least 2 validly
similar jobs (same type, service, metallurgy, size and capacity as applicable) in
past seven years.
c. Supplier for Air Pre-heater of fired heater system should have executed at least
2 validly similar jobs [same type, fuel service as applicable, metallurgy and of at
least 60% of subject APH heat duty (as applicable) and similar fin/stud density
tubes as offered for subject APH] in past 10 years out of which at least one should
be in successful operation for one year. Vendor must provide operation certificate
from the end user of reference APH job.
d. PTR criteria as given in Job Specifications for Electrical, Instrumentation,
Rotating etc.
1.15 Contractor to comply the Owner / EIL list of suppliers as attached in the tender.
For other
items for which no vendor list is available or single vendor is listed, Contractor to ensure
that selected supplier / performer (subcontractor) has PTR for two validly similar supplies
/ activity in last 10 years.
1.16 EXPERIENCE CRITERIA FOR ACTIVITIES:
Refer clause 1.13. Before undertaking any activity in the heater works, Contractor to
ensure that Contractor or his Subcontracting agencies shall meet the Experience Criteria
as given below. Where the Contractor does not meet the experience criteria with respect
to a particular activity for which sub-contracting is allowed, he shall mandatorily employ
the services of Sub-contractor who meets the experience criteria. Services of Sub-
contractors, if approved, shall be necessarily employed for that activity.
It shall be mandatory on the part of Contractor to depute such Sub-contractors and key
personnel as listed in their details submitted during their approval by Owner / EIL.
Page 7 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
1.16.1 Contractor or his Sub-
contractor should have executed “Coil Fabrication” of the coil
metallurgies applicable for job or higher materials (Refer Clause 1.16.2 for Metallurgy
groupings) in at least one Fired Heater / Boiler / any other Fired Equipment / Waste Heat
Recovery System / Piping in a hydrocarbon service as covered by IBR / ASME Sec. I,
VIII or B31.3 in last ten years. This job should be in operation for a minimum of one year
from the date of commissioning.
1.16.2 Metallurgy grouping - The coil materials are grouped as per following hierarchy (from
lower to higher):
a)
CS
b)
Low Alloy steel (LAS) up to P11
c)
d)
e)
SS
f)
High Ni alloys like HP / Incoloy etc.
Experience of any metallurgy within the group shall qualify for all other metallurgies in
the group.
Experience in lower coil metallurgy group shall not be considered for qualifying to a
higher metallurgy group, however vice versa shall be acceptable.
Contractor or his Subcontractor should have experience of “Structural fabrication,
erection, assembly” of at least one fired heater
system job in last ten years.
1.18 Contractor or his Subcontractor is required to have experience of design and engineering
of APH System / stack erection in minimum one no. of Fired Heater job in last ten years.
1.19 Contractor on his own or through his subcontractor shall have experience of the following
activities:
Residual structural design, detailed engineering, preparation of fabrication
drawings
(ii)
Design / engineering and application of refractory
1.20 Contractor to quote a lump sum price for the complete scope of work & supply as defined
in the tender documents including prices for mandatory spares, fabrication & erection
spares, Third-Party Inspection Charges, assistance during pre-commissioning /
commissioning and PGTR.
Contractor’s quoted Lumpsum price to include arranging mandatory site supervision of
erection, certification for correct installation for APH & Skin TCs by the respective OEMs
(Original Equipment Manufacturers).
Contractor to arrange mandatory site supervision of erection, certification for correct
installation for APH & Skin TCs by the respective OEMs (Original Equipment
Manufacturers). Site supervision for FIMs (Burner oil guns) by OEMs shall be arranged
by BPCL. Vendor to coordinate with OEM supervisors and carry out supervision in their
presence. Vendor to inform BPCL requirement of OEM personnel at site for FIMs at least
1 week in advance for arranging their visit. Per diem supervision expenses for free issue
items (Burner oil guns) shall be paid by Client.
Page 8 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
Per diem supervision expenses for items in Contractor scope shall considered by
Contractor in his lumpsum quotation. No separate payment for supervision expenses of
items in contractor scope will be paid by BPCL. Per diem supervision expenses for free
issue items shall be paid by Client.
1.21 For complete scope of work and supply, fabrication, erection, pre-commissioning /
commissioning spares shall be worked out by the Contractor and shall be included within
the quoted Lump sum price.
1.22 Free issue materials to Contractor are defined in the clause no. 3.2 and other discipline
documents referred therein. All balance items required for completion of the revamp of
Heater system as per the tender attachments are in Contractor scope. In case of any
ambiguity clarification to be obtained by the Contractor at Pre bid stage. No clarification
/ cost implication on this account shall be entertained at post award stage and decision
of Owner / PMC shall be final.
1.23 Since during pre-shut down (PSD) the revamp activities are to be taken up in a running
plant, all necessary barricading, safety related precautions, tools & tackles shall be the
Contractor’s responsibility.
All
the OWNER’s safety recommendations, HSE
requirements and all applicable rules & regulations shall be complied to by the
Contractor.
Contractor to note that during Pre shut down, hot work may be permitted with compliance
of safety requirements / local barricading isolation. Owner’s approval for the same shall
be obtained before proceeding for hot work.
Inspection at contractor’s shop and within refinery premise to be carried out by TPI
engaged by the contractor at his own cost. This includes all the inspection activities
during the Pre TA & TA. The TPI agency engaged will be as per the approved TPI list
from BPCL/EIL. List of Inspection agencies for TPI is attached in commercial portion of
tender.
1.25 Spares, as per clause 6.0 shall be supplied by Contractor and shall be included in lump
sum price.
1.26 Procurement and supply of all external insulation wherever required for complete scope
is also is in scope of contractor.
1.27 Contractor must identify and take possession of free issue materials ensuring adequacy
with respect to quality & quantity of the same. Any discrepancy must be brought to the
notice of the owner at the earliest possible so that the overall completion schedule is not
affected. Contractor to ensure proper covering of all FIMs to save it from rain/water
ingress using suitable means (tarpaulin/plastic cover) arranged by contractor at no extra
cost.
In case of any kind of damage to the free issued items (once handed over and accepted
by contractor) on account of improper handling, storage etc. which makes it unfit for use
in its intended purpose as per specification and requirements, shall be repair / rectified /
replaced by Contractor without time and cost implication to Owner.
1.28 Restoration, preservation and protection of existing equipment and their accessories
shall be in Contractor’s scope of work. In case of any damage during, the same shall be
rectified and reinstalled and made in working order to the satisfaction of Engineer-in-
charge.
Page 9 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
1.29 Residual engineering / detailing on account of modular erection and design calculations
to ensure safe lifting, transit and erection of modules at site is in Contractor scope. This
should be applicable for the items to be dismantled and re-erected as requirement e.g.
radiant tubes, tube supports, APH system including ducts etc.
1.30 Complete utility piping items routing modification (as applicable on account of APH
replacement / other local modifications in existing connections) including Hookup with
existing lines, supply, fabrication, supporting, testing and installation is in contractor
scope. Isometrics drawings for the same are to be prepared by Contractor and submitted
for Owner / EIL approval after award. Contractor to obtain further clarity for battery limit
during site visit.
1.31 Inspection Record Documentation
Erection of the equipment shall be done as per the OEM procedures and compliance to
the permitted tolerances as defined in supplier specifications / tender specifications.
The details of all such records shall be maintained and shall be produced to Owner /
PMC on request. These consolidated records shall be finally submitted to Owner for
records.
The Cost for such documentation has to be included in the lump sum price.
1.32 Assistance as required during check listing of revamped heater system, pre-
commissioning and commissioning shall be provided by the Contractor and shall be
included in the lump sum price.
1.33 All Crane requirements for complete job shall be arranged by Contractor.
1.34 Bought out materials except free issue materials defined in tender are in the Contractor
’s
scope. These items shall have identification marking supported by manufacturers test
certificate and must be in compliance with PMS / VMS requirements. Contractor to
submit datasheet of valves and take approval from EIL / BPCL.
1.35 Contractor to refer GA drawings attached with tender along with GA drawing Notes
(C238-131-80-44-4001) for these drawings for information and compliance.
1.36 Contractor to maximize pre shut down work for new equipment work at shop / storage
area at refinery to ensure complete revamp the heater system is completed within shut
down time. Detailed break up schedule for each activity shall be prepared and submitted
with sufficient play of min
days for completion of all identified work prior to shut down.
Contractor to submit detailed schedule with dates (minimum L3) within two weeks from
award of PO.
1.37 All ducting, steel and mating items shall be
and tried at shop for correct
alignment before dispatch to site to avoid any alignment / mating mismatches during
actual erection / installation. All Castable lined pieces shall be
at
shop before dispatch to site.
1.38 Contractor to note that adequate commissioning and erection spares & consumables
need to be considered in his scope of supply. Also, any dismantled & temporary items
which are not required in final system shall be cleared from the site and disposed safely
as per Owner requirements (refer elsewhere) and Owner shall not accept any such items
after the job is complete. Site shall be cleared of such temporary material and
construction debris.
Page 10 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
1.39 Contractor to perform residual detailing of the APH system including existing APH
support structure modification based on
supplier’s
APH to be procured by the contractor.
Indicative sketch for the APH modifications may be referred by the contractor.
It is Contractor’s responsibility to ensure that the dimensions of procured APHs are such
that the same can be retrofitted in place of existing APH by local modification of the APH
support structure, connected ducts & associated local modifications. Existing APH
interface flange dimensions can be considered during fresh APH procurement itself to
the extent possible. Also ensuring safety, crane access, operation, maintenance and
replacement access and ease provisions as per proven and good engineering practices
while finalizing the revamped APH system arrangement is Contractor’s responsibility.
The Contractor shall submit the updated Air preheater system drawings incorporating
the above requirements for EIL’s review and all comments shall be incorporated without
any cost / time delay in schedule. Contractor to also note that the removal of various
” marked in the EIL’s layout drawings is the responsibility of Contractor.
1.40 Contractor shall prepare and submit documents & drawings as per vendor data
requirement VDR (refer discipline wise VDR) attached with the tender.
1.41 The ODC transportation criteria shall be as follows: -
a.
Equipment up to dimensions of 12 M (L) x 3 M (W) x 3 M (H) inclusive of length
for all Nozzle & Saddle Projections and weight up to 32 MT shall be transported
from anywhere in India by Road to BPCL-MR Refinery on Mechanical Trailers.
b.
Equipment of dimensions 12M to 25 M (L) x 3M to 5.5 M (W) x 4.5 M (H) inclusive
of length for all Nozzle & Saddle Projections and weight up to 110 MT shall be
Transported from anywhere in India by Road to BPCL-MR Refinery on Hydraulic
Axle Trailers.
1.42 Contractor shall understand and verify at site before proceeding wrt the structural
sections and dimensions at the major interface locations with existing process coils, tube
supports, structure, platforms, ladders, utility piping and ducts etc.
1.43 Contractor to note basic clash check based on available information & drawings has
been done while preparing the Revamp GA drawings for new equipment / APH system.
Contractor
a thorough check for feasibility of layout for any clashes /
fouling of new revamp system with existing equipment / Pipe rack / platform / structural
members / cables / instruments /utility lines etc. and consider these modifications as part
of the scope with information to Owner / EIL. Any supply / activities required on account
of this are part of Contractor scope.
1.44 All affected existing instruments coming on existing radiant / convection / ducts / process
coils which are to be dismantled should be taken out carefully in shut down and kept
safely for re-installation of the revamped radiant / convection arrangement / new APH
system ducting.
ALL ELECTRICAL ITEMS CABLES CABLES
Page 11 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
The existing heater is a Multicellular box type Vertical tube heater. There is total of 272
tubes in the radiant section distributed in 8 fluid passes and 4 cells (without any wall
partition). The convection section is located on top of the radiant section.
Each radiant cell has 6 floor mounted dual fired (Fuel oil, Fuel gas and off-gas) burners,
i.e. total 24 burners in 4 cells, arranged in a staggered fashion.
The heater has a balanced draft system (Outboard Cast and glass type APH with two
FD fans and one ID fan). The heater shares a common structure mounted stack with the
adjoining VDU heater.
In brief the purpose of the revamp is: -
Increasing the number of tubes per pass in radiant section by adding 8 nos. of
new 6” radiant tubes per pass.
Addition of 2 row of tubes (8 tubes per row) in the existing future tube rows of the
convection section.
Replacement of existing Cast & glass type APH with latest design CAPH
& GAPH (as per new APH datasheet) for increased thermal duty and reliability.
Replacing all existing 24 nos. of oil guns with new oil guns.
Associated modifications in radiant coil / cross over / terminals / spigots, tube
supports, radiant casing / floor / arch and refractory modifications.
Associated APH system modification including APH support structure
modification.
Associated convection section / convection coil / convection terminal
modification.
Associated skin thermocouple modification & other instrument related
modification.
Relocation / replacement of radiant inlet / outlet sleeves as per new arrangement
and making provision for installation of tube supports (wall tube hangers & top
supports) with necessary steel (floor, wall & arch) modifications.
Bolts / washers as required for installation of tube supports need to be procured
by contractor in adequate length and numbers including for re used supports
considering revamp.
Heater contractor is responsible on single point basis for completing all activities defined
in tender for the revamp of the heater. For detailed scope of supply and work, contractor
shall refer the tender documents thoroughly.
Contractor to refer this specification along with the list of attachment of tender index
(Doc. No. C238-131-80-44-MC-T-7401) for understanding the complete scope and
requirements of the project. Any item not specifically stated but required for completion
of the job is considered in Contractor scope.
Heater contractor to note that time is the essence of this Project and the work has to be
completed within shut down (for shut down duration refer commercial section) and
handed over to Owner for commissioning activities. Contractor shall understand the
actual amount of work involved and prepare a detailed step wise execution philosophy
post award in line with defined methodology with suggestive improvements if any and
should discuss the same for Owner / EIL comments during Kick of meeting.
Page 12 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
BPCL KR shall procure and issue at site storage area following items of the heater
system for Contractor for pre shut down work and their erection during pre shut down /
shut down as per the execution philosophy.
shall be issued as free issue
by Owner and all balance items need to be taken care in his scope by contractor to make
the revamp job complete. Considering revamp job Contractor shall identify all activities
& items which shall be required for execution / completeness and should be ready with
back up resources / manpower for meeting last minute gaps reported if any. Contractor
shall obtain clarification (if any) prior to bidding.
Free issue item:
Burners oil guns as per attached MR
Copy of the MR is attached with Tender for information of the Contractor. Final selected
supplier OEM documents shall be provided to the Heater contractor as per the MR
provisions and at appropriate earliest stage. Other discipline scope document also to be
referred by Bidder for complete clarity. Overall completeness of material and services for
the job completion is Contractor’s responsibility.
Heater pressure parts as per the attached list
BPCL shall be providing heater pressure parts as FIM, as per the attached list. The
contractor must thoroughly study the job scope including engineering drawings to ensure
the completeness of material required. Any additional material required based on the
detailed engineering or during the job execution will be in the vendor’s scope. Overall
completeness of material and services for the job completion is Contractor’s
responsibility.
For utility supply (electricity, water etc.) by Owner, Contractor shall refer commercial
section of the tender. No utility material will be supplied by BPCL. All materials except
FIMs is in contractor’s scope.
For clarity purpose detailed list of activities has been split by identifying Pre-Shutdown
(PSD) / Pre fabrication work and SD (shut down) work which shall help in maximizing
PSD/ Pre fabrication work work and minimizing actual shut down time. Contractor can
supplement these activities in his execution philosophy including all minor details to make
the job complete during shut down. Contractor to also refer revamp GA drawings for
better understanding of sequence of activities.
Pressure part dismantling / replacement with new pre-fabricated coils minimizing site
weld philosophy is the focus of the job. Parallel working in all 4 heater cells is to be
considered to adhere to short shut down period.
Execution philosophy with suggested minimum field welds is indicated in Arrangement
drawings for Contractor’s reference. Any improvement in philosophy based on resources
deployed to meet overall shut down period.
Contractor to consider and prepare methodology based on his APH procurement
schedule for the existing APH replacement within or subsequent to shut down with heater
system operating in FD mode. System enabling activities during shut down is in
contractor scope for this APH replacement in case it is required to be done during FD
Page 13 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
mode heater system operation. APH system redundant isolation in flue gas path / air
path in addition to isolation guillotine dampers & control dampers needs to be put in place
during shut down itself.
Intimating Owner / PMC at least 4 weeks in advance for FD mode operation of system
to be done by contractor without fail for necessary preparedness and support to
contractor at site.
Heater contractor shall update bought out details as per the bought-out information
received in order to complete the residual engineering work and fabrication details in
fabrication drawings. Balance technical details if any as required for fabrication &
detailing are in Contractor scope and shall be developed by the Contractor and submitted
for Owner / EIL review.

text.b: Pre shut down / pre fabrication work: (All hot work only after Owner approval)

page.text: Collection and safe storage of free issue items.
Procurement and Safe storage of balance bought out items.
Necessary local barricading / scaffolding to carry out.
APH area modification as per requirement from BPCL as per applicable drawing and
directions of EIC.
Pre fabrication (with maximum modularization such as preparation of coil U pins as per
the contractor’s final approved execution methodology)
and safe storage of radiant coils,
convection coil, crossover, jump over piping, coil fittings, radiant & convection terminal /
transfer line piping, utility piping (as applicable) etc.
Safe Storage of all pre-fabricated new duct parts CAD, HAD, CFG & HFG ducting
portions including hoppers & transition duct on account of new APH (CAPH + GAPH).
The detail to be developed by the Contractor based on the new APH bought out detail
for integration with existing duct system with minimum modifications.
All new instrument nozzles shall be pre-installed in new duct pieces / terminal pipes.
Castable lined ducts shall be supplied in lined and pre dried and stored at site ready for
erection during shut down
Painting work as possible during pre-shutdown as per painting specification attached in
tender.
Any other work identified and permitted by BPCL as per instruction of Engineer-In-
Charge that may be planned during pre-shutdown activities.
Contractor shall refer respective discipline specifications for detailed activities. To
achieve completion of complete work within shut down period, it is imperative for
contractor to understand and mobilize multiple teams / gangs simultaneously in different
part of system. Mobilization of adequate resources with past experience of similar work
shall eliminate any rework and issues during short time execution with perfection.
Individual teams can be formulated for simultaneous work by Contractor during SD.
Ensuring personnel, equipment safety as per owner procedure during all time is
imperative and should be highlighted in execution methodology.
Various major activity details are listed below
for contractor’s information. Contractor to
detail out further based on revamp scope including minor activities in his execution
philosophy:
Page 14 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved

text.b: Radiant coil / Crossover / radiant coil terminal / floor / Casing / arch / refractory

page.text: - Temporary scaffolding as required.
- Temporary supporting of the existing radiant coils identified for dismantling and
relocation inside the radiant section as identified in the revamp GAD.
- Cutting of the welds between radiant internal coils and radiant crossover / radiant HC
outlet terminal portion to free the radiant internal coils for modification as identified in the
revamp GAD.
- Removal of the tube support for these radiant coils.
- Arch portion local modification including refractory as applicable.
- Cutting and relocation of the radiant coils as identifies in the revamp GAD. Temporary
supporting the relocated coils till the connection with adjacent coils and permanent tube
supporting is done.
- Installation of new radiant coils inside the radiant section, provide temporary supporting
and complete the modified coil arrangement including bends etc. as per revamp GAD.
For the installation of the new tubes, temporary cut out in the radiant casing can be made
as an alternate to tube pulling door. Contractor to explore further option based on their
execution philosophy.
- Installation of new tube supports including re-installation of relocated tube supports.
- Radiant crossover / radiant HC outlet terminal line / radiant HC outlet transfers line
portion to suit the change in radiant inlet / outlet location due to the addition of new
radiant coils. Contractor to ensure that the Heater side flange orientation for the modified
coil arrangement is matching the interface with existing counter flange on the radiant HC
outlet transfer line side.
- PWHT / NDT / RT of the new coil welds.
- New radiant coil anchor installation along with associated radiant wall modification.
- External insulation of modified crossovers / radiant HC outlet terminal line / radiant HC
outlet transfers line portion. For crossovers, the current insulation (combined insulation
for all 8 passes) will have to be completely replaced on account of modification in the
crossover portion.
- Welding of new radiant coil spigots.
- Radiant floor / floor refractory modification including radiant spigot sleeve installation /
TSK sleeve installation as per new coil layout.
- Radiant wall modification including casing / TSK sleeve installation / refractory as
identified in revamp GADs.
- Radiant floor / floor refractory modification to add one new access door in each cell, i.e.
4 access doors in total.
- Replacement of existing tube skin thermocouples with new skin thermocouples along
with installation of new additional thermocouples as per revamp GAD.
- Temporary scaffolding as required.
- Cutting of the welds between convection HC coils inlet and convection inlet HC terminal
coil portion to free the convection coils for modification as identified in the revamp GAD.
- Modification of the convection inlet HC transfer line portion including necessary existing
transfer line support modification to match it with the new increased convection HC coil
inlet elevation as per the revamp GAD.
- Removal of the convection header box panels as required and the header box
modification (for increased header box width locally) as per revamp GAD to suit the
increased projection of new jump overs from ETS.
- Insertion of new convection tubes in the future rows of the convection section.
- Welding of new TSK on convection shock tubes.
- Installation of new convection jump over and new bends on the tube pulling side.
Page 15 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
- Modification of existing convection inlet terminal coil portion to account for increased
convection HC inlet terminal elevation.
- PWHT / NDT / RT of the new coil bends.
- Installing anchors at new HC convection inlet, connection of modified HC convection inlet
terminals with modified convection HC inlet transfer line as per revamp GAD / isometrics.
External insulation of the terminals / modified HC inlet transfer line portion is also be
provided as per revamp GAD.
- Header box panels re-installation.
- Hydrotesting of the Heater coils (convection + radiant) after completion of the Process
piping revamp in the heater / transfer line as per the hydrotest pressure specified in the
revamp GAD. The calibrated pressure gauge (min 2 nos.) of proper range as per code
shall be used for hydro test. All joints shall subject to hydro testing. However, those joints
for which hydro testing is not practically possible shall be treated as golden joints.
- Balance electrical, instrumentation, piping work, if any defined elsewhere in the tender.
Dismantling of existing APH (Cast + Glass type), modification of existing APH support
structure modification, erection work for new APH (Cast + Glass type), seal welding,
related duct modification and external insulation
- Barricading and scaffolding (as required) to carry out the work in APH area.
- Safekeeping activities as applicable including existing instruments etc. which will be re-
installed suitably after the main modifications as per the execution philosophy.
- Removal of existing HGF ducts portion including hopper to create free space for APH
dismantling.
- Removal of existing cold / hot air ducts portion including hopper portion to create free
space for APH dismantling.
- Removal of connection between existing APH and CFG duct hopper.
- Module wise dismantling of CAPH + GAPH + intermediate duct + return duct (hoppers).
- CFG duct hopper dismantling along with portion of connected CFG duct as applicable.
- Existing APH structure modification including platform to suit for new APH
(CAPH+GAPH) with new dimensions and different supporting location / arrangement as
per approved Contractor’ APH structure drawings to suit new APH configuration
- New refractory lined and dried out CFG duct portion installation including new CFG duct
hopper to match new APH dimensions.
- New CAPH + GAPH installation module wise along with connected refractory lined and
dried out vertical flue gas duct (connecting piece between GAPH & CAPH matching the
new APH dimensions) / return ducts (hoppers).
- New refractory lined and dried out HFG duct portion installation including new HFG duct
hopper to match new APH dimensions.
- New air duct portions (cold air and hot air) including new hoppers to match new APH
dimensions.
- This new APH system modification shall be done as
per approved Contractor’s revamp
APH system drawing to suit new APH configuration.
- Insulation of the new APH + CFG ducts + return ducts (hoppers)+ hot air ducts as
applicable.
- Balance platform, hand rail, ladder modification as applicable.
- Connection of APH skin thermocouple and other instruments installation / modification
as required.
- Utility piping (water wash / LP steam / drain lines etc.) local modification as required as
per P&ID and approved isometrics.
Refer instrumentation specifications for instrument related modification details. Impulse
piping for all instruments from taping points till instruments as per applicable PMS and
Page 16 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
standards. Please refer instrumentation specification C238-131-16-51-1001 for further
details.
Instrumentation / Electrical scope for DCS & Electrical revamps
Procurement, inspection and testing, supply, installation & commissioning of field
instruments, Supply and laying of instrumentation cables and cable trays along with fire
proofing of the same for all the field instrumentation items, which are in Contracto
r’s
scope, Procurement and installation of electrical items coming on the heater and being
removed and refixed as part of the heater revamp jobs are in the contractor
s scope. The
required electrical fitting needs to be procured and supplied and is in the scope of the
contractor.
Any other work identified and permitted by BPCL as per instruction of Engineer-In-
Charge may be planned as part of activities.
Open all man ways of heater for internal inspection (Radiant, Convection, Stack).
Provide sufficient lighting inside the heater.
Air Hoses / Ejectors shall be provided on the man ways of the heaters.
Air / Water / Steam hoses, fire retardant clothes, fire extinguishers for carrying out hot
works.
Inspection & painting activities.
Fire proofing of APH support structures (modified portions) up to EL+ 9.1m as per OISD
/ attached EIL Standard Specification 6-68-0033.
Instrumentation / electrical scope as applicable.
Existing heater structure modification as applicable.
Dismantling of temporary structures and barricading
Statutory approvals assistance to Owner.
All necessary scaffolding work required to execute the revamp
Re installation of dismantled instruments of radiant / convection / transfer line / existing
ducts along with blind flanges etc.
Providing assistance in pre commissioning / commissioning and PGTR as per the
instructions of the Engineer in charge & OEM supervision.
Any balance work from pre-shutdown and shutdown
activities due to any unforeseen situation, to be executed by contractor post shutdown
without any extra cost. Contractor to ensure completion of all activities during pre-
shutdown and shutdown period.
The Contractor shall consider and prepare a detailed methodology, aligned with their
APH procurement schedule, for replacement of the existing APH either during the
shutdown or subsequent to the shutdown with the heater system operating in FD mode.
In case the APH replacement is required to be executed with the heater operating in FD
mode, all system-
enabling activities during the shutdown shall be within the Contractor’s
scope.
Redundant isolation of the APH system in the flue-gas path and air path, in addition to
isolation guillotine dampers and control dampers, shall be implemented during the
shutdown itself.
Page 17 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
Refer above MR (in Clause 3.2) for item being supplied as free issue. Other major items
in contractor scope are listed for Contractor convenience.
Other than the free issue item, the procurement, supply and fabrication, in sequence and
at the appropriate time, of all materials and consumable required for completion of work
in all respects for the heater systems revamp shall be entirely the responsibility of the
Contractor and his quoted price shall include for all such materials / facilities. Contractor's
scope of supply items shall consist of all the items along with spares as defined in
drawings, standards & specifications, without being limited to them. Any item not
expressly stated but required for completion of the job is considered in Contractor scope.
All procurement of related items shall be done by the Contractor and the quoted base
price shall include for the same.
Major supply items are described below for contractor’s reference:
Floor Plate local modification at changed coil locations towards radiant inlet / outlet walls
to accommodate new sleeves along with structural stiffeners, floor access door as per
the revamp GAD.
Floor refractory for the local radiant floor modification
New floor spigot sleeves for new / modified radiant coils.

text.b: Radiant wall / Arch section / Radiant coils

page.text: New radiant coils, bends and fittings as per revamp GADs.
- New radiant crossover coils and fittings (including external insulation as per revamp
GAD), new radiant HC inlet terminal coils, radiant HC inlet transfer line coils portion
including flanges, gaskets, bolts, nuts etc., instrument nozzles including valves, flanges,
gaskets, nuts etc. as applicable, coil sleeves, sealing arrangement, terminal anchor /
support arrangement as per revamp GAD. For crossovers, the current insulation
(combined insulation for all 8 passes) will have to be completely replaced on account of
modification in the crossover portion.
New coil spigot & spigot sleeves (part of radiant floor) as per revamp GAD.
Radiant wall / arch casing plate, as applicable, on account of damages / local
modifications during execution.
Radiant structural steel local modification as applicable.
Radiant wall / arch refractory and refractory anchors, as applicable, on account of
damages / local modifications during execution.
New radiant tube supports as per revamp GAD.
Auxiliary instruments modification, as applicable.
New radiant skin thermocouples along with further connections as applicable. Refer
instrumentation scope in this regard.
New convection coils (plain + studded) including jumpovers, fittings etc. as per revamp
GAD, instrument nozzle including valves, flanges, gaskets, nuts etc. as applicable, coil
sleeves, sealing arrangement, coil anchor arrangement (as required)
New convection HC coil inlet terminals / modified convection HC coil inlet transfer line
piping (including insulation as per revamp GAD) along with fittings, flanges, gaskets,
nuts, studs, pad plates, structural members, saddles etc.
Page 18 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
Modified portion of refractory lined Header box.
Auxiliary instruments, as applicable.
New convection skin thermocouples.
New pre-fabricated cold air duct portion including hopper to connect existing cold air duct
with new APH (CAPG + GAPH type ) matching the new APH dimensions.
New pre-fabricated hot air duct portion including hopper to connect existing hot air duct
with new APH (CAPH + GAPH type) matching the new APH dimensions
Cold flue gas (CFG) and hot flue gas (HFG) ducting including hopper with refractory
lining along with retainer plate / external insulation and pre dry out to connect existing
ducts with new APH (CAPH + GAPH type) matching the new APH dimensions.
APH air / flue gas duct flanges / hoppers connecting flanges.
New External insulation on modified hot air duct portion including hopper
New External insulation on CFG ducts modified portion including hopper.
Modified APH support structure portion based on new APH (CAPH + GAPH type)
arrangement as per approved
Contractor’s
APH system modification drawings,
Platform grating / hand rail for modified platforms as applicable (including modified APH
support structure platform) and modification of existing platforms and ladders, as
applicable.
New APH (CAPH + GAPH type) complete with modules and transition pieces, return
headers, water washing arrangement, mandatory spares, specialized tools / tackles, etc.
External insulation
Companion flanges for air and flue gas flanges of APH, gasket and bolting
Connecting air and flue gas ducts / hopper
Seal welding consumables for modules
Studs, gaskets and nuts as per APH supplier drawing
Utility piping local modification as applicable.
New APH TSK connection

text.b: Refractory / Insulating material / Refractory tie back / Auxiliary materials (As

page.text: All refractory and insulation materials like ceramic fiber blanket / modules / board /
vacuum formed blocks, refractory fire brick, acid resistance Bricks, castable,
mortars, rock-wool, mineral wool, SS foil etc. as per drawings, standards and
specifications.
All types and shapes of tie back materials (SS 304/310/321/304L/316L) like V/Y
lugs, retainer plates, shelf flats, chain link, stud for cup locks, speed fix washers,
ceramic cup locks, moldable, etc.
Welding equipment and consumable such as welding rods for welding of tie backs
etc.
Miscellaneous materials like 0.1 to 0.15mm thick SS 316 foil, Polyethylene sheets,
tape, rope and fiber, grease, heat resistant adhesive, tar paper, mortars, ceramic
fiber for gap packing, ceramic coating etc. required for execution and completion of
the job.
Page 19 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
All tube support castings and bolting required for radiant section. The tube support
bolt lengths should also account for any additional stiffening plate on heater casing
over and above as per EIL standard requirement.
As per the scope of supply & work defined as per instrumentation Job specification
C238-131-16-51-SP-1001 attached with the Tender document.
i)
Primer, heat resistance paint on hot surfaces and final painting of heater and APH
system, epoxy resin paint for ceramic fiber lined surface.
ii)
Welding consumable.
iii)
Sealing cloth for opening around tube sleeves suitable for high temperature
application.
iv) Galvanized gratings (Type-II) as per EIL Standard 7-68-0697 with holding down
clamps.
v)
All fittings, sleeves (CS & SS), valves and flanges for instruments and auxiliary
connection on heater.
vi) Various grades of SS materials in steel work comprising of items like retainer
plates, Shelf flats, support plates etc.
vii) All bolting materials (including SS/Alloy bolts for coil supports) as per drawings,
standards.
viii) All Cross Overs / Process Piping / Utility Piping supports including pad plates,
pipes, Structural members and saddles etc.
ix) Pipes and fittings for heater coil shall consist of:
Plain tubes
Studded tubes
WBW fittings (return bends, elbows, reducers etc.)
Forged fittings (weldolets, elbolets etc.)
Forged flanges
Consumables such as welding electrodes, filler wires etc. with weld coupon
materials
Gaskets, studs / nuts
Valves for Instrument connections on coil
Pad plate / half coupling / plugs etc.
x)
All testing / recording equipment / consumable, e.g. radiography, ultrasonic testing,
PMI, dye penetration, PWHT machine & resources, hardness machine etc.
xi) All tools, tackles, cranes etc. required to execute the job.
xii)
Transportation from Owner’s store, assembly / erection of FIMs
Page 20 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
xiii) All arrangement for compressed air for flushing of coil after hydro-testing / cleaning.
xiv) Personnel protection requirements during execution phase.
xv) All instrument installation piping materials like pipes, fittings, flanges, valves,
gaskets etc.
xvi) Supply of splitters / guide vanes etc. (specially 2 nos. min. for all inlet hopper duct)
Any other item not specifically listed in these specifications but are required to complete
the job in all respects shall also be supplied by the contractor within his quoted rates.
All drawings, standards and specifications referred to in the List of contents in the
technical portion of tender, document no. C238-131-80-44-MC-T-7401 shall be
applicable for this job.
The drawings enclosed with tender documents are good for engineering. This being a
Revamp job Contractor is required to also refer the drawings of existing heaters provided
in Tender. Based on these drawings, contractor shall carry out balance detailing based
on the bought-out items detailing and shall prepare detailed fabrication drawings and
submit the same for EIL approval as per Vendor Data Requirement (VDR).
5.1.1 The exact design details about auxiliary equipment like APHs (contractor supply items)
are not available and hence ducting routing, various elevations and details of APH
system are not finalized.
It is Contractor’s responsibility to ensure that the dimensions of procured APH are such
that the new APH (CAPH + GAPH) is accommodated within the existing APH supported
columns with local modification in existing APH structure / platform / gratings / handrails
/ bracings / connected ducting / instruments / utilities. Also ensuring safety, crane access,
operation, maintenance and replacement access and ease provisions as per proven and
good engineering practices while finalizing the modified APH system arrangement is
Contractor’s responsibility. Necessary approach / headroom shall be ensured by the
contractor while finalizing details.
5.1.2 Coil alignment and erection work to be done with utmost care and all precaution to be
taken, proper welding sequence, cold pull, counter weight supports and aligning etc. so
that final coil alignment achieved shall be within limits specified in specification 6-17-
5.1.3 For procurement of all pressure parts like studded, plain tubes, fittings, etc, the Heater
vendor shall compile the BOM and Item description from the drawings attached with the
tender.
5.1.4 External insulation of the cross-over/terminals shall
also be in Contractor’s scope.
5.1.5 In order to prevent entrance of extraneous material during fabrication, coil assemblies
shall be blown out with compressed air and end capped during storage/interruption
during fabrication. After erection, coil connection openings to be provided with blind
flanges to prevent ingress of foreign material / rain water.
Page 21 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
5.1.6 Only electric resistance coil should be used for pre-heating/ post weld heating of alloy
steel welding.
5.1.7 All nozzle welds in CS and Cr-MO steels shall be examined by magnetic particle
examination.
5.1.8 Radiography of the bottom return bends of the vertical radiant coils to check for foreign
debris shall be done just prior to final hydrotest of coil and shall
be considered as “Hold
Point” for Final Hydro test.
5.1.9 Contractor has to install structural members, platform, platform supporting members,
handrails, Utility piping supporting members, stiffeners etc. Necessary hot work
approvals shall be obtained from Owner complying to safety procedures / requirements.
5.1.10 The Contractor shall submit a detailed construction / fabrication / erection methodology
immediately based on the clause 3.3 after the award of Contract,
. The Contractor shall inform the modules considered along with their size
and weight.
5.1.11 The Contractor shall prepare and submit the fabrication drawings with complete design
and details and also including overall dimension, weight, lifting lugs, temporary lifting /
stiffening / transportation aids, etc.
MODULARIZATION / TRIAL ASSEMBY
5.2.1 Trial assembly of the modules shall be carried out by the Contractor at the fabrication
yard before dispatch of the modules to site.
REFRACTORY
5.3.1 All refractory and Insulation work shall be carried out as per the tender specifications /
standards for Refractory lining.
5.3.2 Contractor shall submit the pre dry-out procedure (duly vetted by refractory supplier) and
schedule for review / approval of OWNER / EIL for shop dry out. Pre dry out temperature
to be considered for
CFG and HFG ducts shall be as per GA notes (C238-131-80-44-
5.3.3 The refractory materials which have a shelf life, should have at least 6 month of
remaining shelf life on arrival at site.

text.b: 5.3.4 Refractory dry out of all refractory lined segments shall be done before

page.text: 5.3.5 Anchor spacing for lining shall be as per tender requirement.
Cast & Glass APH shall be designed and supplied to comply with the requirements of
Datasheets and EIL Job specifications and their attachments.
For all bought out item erection, the Contractor shall ensure erection as per OEM
recommended procedures and requirements.
Contractor to ensure that utility piping and its support (as applicable) shall be installed in
such a manner that head room clearance is available and free movement on
platform/grade is not obstructed.
Page 22 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
Only SS shims to be used for equipment alignment if at all required.
Tube sealing arrangements shall
be of proven manufacturer’s standard suitable for flue
gas temp. & composition and expansions requirement.
For painting requirements refer EIL specification 6-79-0020 / Owners spec and
environment shall be considered as corrosive environment.
5.10 Color coding shall be carried out as per Client specification, which shall be given to
Contractor post-order by the Engineer-in-charge (Site).
5.11 Finalization of WPS / PQR shall be Contractor
’s responsibility. Material required along
with the consumables shall be in Contractor
’s scope.
It is Contractor’s responsibility to ensure that a
ll erected operating mechanisms required
as part of the heater tender shall be checked for free and desired operation without any
obstruction and shall be reported in case of any restriction etc.
5.13 Application of primers, intermediate and final painting including surface preparation,
color coding and letter writing etc. on heater, ducting system and the complete structure
including ladders and stairs as required shall be done as per the painting requirement
attached with tender.
5.14.1 All items and bought out equipment in Contractor
’s scope fall under Third party
Inspection. Scope of inspection as a minimum shall be as defined in relevant Job /
Standard specification, ITPs attached with the tender and as given below. Contractor
shall prepare and submit ITPs for all items and submit for EIL review / approval after
obtaining TPIA approval.
The cost of TPIA engaged for inspection of bought out
equipment shall be borne by the contractor.
Structural steel plates, profiles etc.
C
Standard tubes / fittings / pipes, fasteners,
etc.
C
Gaskets
C
Castings, Studded tubes.
C
APH, Burners, Skin thermocouples etc.
C
Refractory Materials
B
Fabrication and Erection work at shop
A
Fabrication and assembly work at Site
A
Erection, testing & other site work
A
Legend:
A :
Stage wise and final inspection with document review
B :
Final inspection and document review
Page 23 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
C :
Document review
Material identification shall be done at
supplier’s shop and at
site as per PMI specification
listed in Tender
5.14.3 The quality and inspection plan of the Contractor shall comply with all the requirements
in the tender.
5.15 HYDROTESTING
Apart from final hydro testing of assembled radiant and convection tubes, each
convection studded tubes shall be hydro tested at ground after welding of bridging
pieces, 50 mm extra length may be considered on each side of tube for hydro testing
which shall be cut to final length as per drawing. Complete water removal after hydro
testing shall be done from heater coils. Wherever complete elimination of water from
coils are not possible, Contractor may propose alternatives to hydro-testing subject to
Owner / EIL approval.
5.16 PRESERVATION
All material supplies and bought-out equipment shall be supplied with proper wrapping
and packing, so that they will be preserved in as new condition during handling and under
the rainy/ corrosive outdoor condition of storage for a minimum period of 6 months.
Contractor shall submit equipment manuals / data folder which shall contain storage/
preservation, handling, erection instructions.
5.17 ERECTION OF CRITICAL EQUIPMENT
Contractor shall arrange for Site supervision during erection along with certification for
correct installation by the Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) of the APH, Burner
components & Skin TCs.
Compliance to above requirements shall be ensured and documentary proof for the
same shall be submitted by Contractor and duly verified by EIL-Site before Contractor /
EIL
Site raises call for Final Site-Check-listing by Heater Specialist from EIL H.O.
FABRICATION & ERECTION SPARES
6.1.1 Fabrication / erection / pre-commissioning / commissioning spares (over & above the
mandatory spares) including material for welder’s qualification shall be worked out by
the Bidders and shall be included as quantities additional to the actual requirements. The
price of these shall be included in the total lumpsum price.
These spares are not required to be deposited to the OWNER’s stores, but should be
available with Contractor at site as and when required, to avoid delays during site
execution.
6.1.3 Any leftover items will not be taken by the OWNER and site shall be cleared of all such
items at the completion of the job.
MANDATORY SPARES
Page 24 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
The following mandatory spares shall be in the Contractor’s scope of supply. The price
of mandatory spares as defined below shall be included in lumpsum price. These items
shall be procured and handed over to Owner:

text.b: Heater wise mandatory spares rounded off to next higher integer

page.text: Gaskets
: 400% (for pressure parts excluding the terminal
flanges)
100% (for other locations except flange joints of
ducts)
Boltings (Pressure parts)
Pressure parts (Incl. all type of
tubes, fittings, flanges, elbowlets,
weldolets
etc.
but
excluding
manifold and its flanges and end
caps)
: 5 % (Minimum 1 no. in each type, size &
material along with welding consumables)
Tube support casting / assemblies /
twin tube guide (except tube sheet)
: 3 % (Minimum 1 2 no. in each type, size &
material)
Tube Skin Thermocouples
20 % or Min. one no. skin point Thermocouple
for each type-length, material and for each
orientation with rounding off to the nearest
integer.
APH
Bolting & fasteners
All
Gaskets
including
return
headers
Bolting & fasteners
Glass Tube
5% (min. 50 nos.)
Bush / Glands
5% (min. 50 nos.)
Plug
Twice the no. of spare tubes
The mandatory spares for Radiant and convection tubes shall be supplied in
finished length (with intermediate welds) as per the requirements specified in the
arrangement drawings. For jumpovers, crossovers and coil inlet / outlet terminals,
quantity of tubes shall be in maximum possible length and estimated based on
percentage of total required length.
Contractor to also submit item wise list with unit rates for additional recommended
operational spares for 2 years normal operation over and above the Mandatory Spares
given above, if any. In case no list is provided it is assumed that the Bidder / Contractor
has not recommended any such spares.
Page 25 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
All spares supplied by Heater contractor shall be properly wrapped and packed so that
spares will be preserved in as new condition under the normal condition of storage
envisaged and shall be properly tagged & coded so that at a later stage the same can
be identified. Packing list shall be furnished so that parts can be identified without
uncrating.
Contractor shall guarantee revamped portion of heater components / parts against
defective design and materials, poor workmanship and failure from normal usage for a
period as specified in tender document / GPC. Supplier shall repair or replace, without
charge any defective parts found within that time. This guarantee shall also include sub-
ordered / subcontracted / bought-out items forming part of the total contractor supplies.
If a separate guarantee clause has been specified in procurement specification for items
in Contractor scope of supply, then the stringent of the two shall govern.
Contractor shall guarantee to accept full responsibility for all fabricated components /
parts of the revamped portion of heater system in Contractor's scope of supply /
fabricated by him in regard to selection, fabrication and coordination of the various
components.
Any damage caused to existing heaters / FIMs / bought outs during execution of the job
by Contractor shall be rectified without no cost or time implication to Client.
The period for enforceable guarantee, contingencies and penalties, if any shall be as
defined elsewhere in the Tender Document.
During performance guarantee test run (PGTR) at site, Heater contractor with support
from EIL shall demonstrate the guaranteed performance parameters corresponding to
the Heater design load (77.0 MMKcal / hr) and fuel efficiency (guaranteed min. 90.4% in
fuel oil firing) mentioned in the API datasheet. In an event that unit / CDU-3 Crude heater
operates at a throughput lower than the heater design throughput / condition, the
guaranteed performance parameters (load and fuel efficiency as mentioned above) of
the heater shall be demonstrated for the purpose of guarantee in mutual discussion with
BPCL. PGTR procedure shall be finalized between BPCL and EIL.
Structure or parts thereof and materials entering therein, indicating irremediable or
injurious defects, improper fabrication, excessive repairs or that are not in accordance
with this specification shall be subject to rejection. They shall also be subject to rejection
if such conditions are discovered after acceptance at the fabricator's works or at site.
Completed equipment, parts of equipment, or materials containing defects originating
with the supplier's materials, or workmanship or that are not in complete compliance with
the requirements of the order will be subject to rejection.
Discovery of conditions warranting rejection, after inspection and acceptance of the
equipment by the Purchaser does not relieve the supplier of the responsibility to comply
with the order.
The Contractor is expected to have a strong Quality Assurance system active both at
office and at site. The quality requirements, documentation requirements, formats,
Page 26 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
protocols, procedures defined in the Tender specifications shall be followed by the
Contractor.
Contractor is also required to follow the specifications: 6-78-0001, 6-78-0002 attached
elsewhere in the Tender.
The specification 6-78-0003 shall also be applicable for procurement of items in
Contractor
’s scope.
10.1 Contractor shall furnish drawings / documents /
data for owner’s review / approval
purposes. All drawings & documents shall bear the purchaser's job number, purchase
order, Owner's name, location, item number and service. Proposed execution
methodology shall be submitted along with bids for records.
10.2 Bidders to note that no technical data except qualification documents and filled-in
Technical Bid Compliance Format (C238-131-80-44-OD-02) is required to be submitted
at bid stage.
10.3 It is sole responsibility of the Contractor, that complete details of modification work are
reflected in detail fabrication drawings prepared by him and submitted to Owner / EIL.
Any additional drawing / data thus required shall be furnished by Contractor.
10.4 All data / drawing shall be in English Language and in the Metric system.
10.5 Complete list of drawings / documents with document number, review category and
anticipated submission date must be submitted
after award of the
Contract. Contractor shall also submit a micro level plan for the total activities for revamp
including the total shut down activities. Owner observations shall be incorporated in this
plan, by the contractor, without any time and cost implications.
10.6 The Contractor shall be accountable for furnishing all data / drawings in proper form and
quality. Accountability of sub vendor documentation shall also be with Contractor.
10.7 Contractor to prepare for and attend the kick off meeting within two weeks after award of
contract with the following agenda as a minimum:
i.
Finalization of master document control index with schedule of submission.
ii.
Presentation of detailed execution methodology by Contractor and its
finalization.
iii.
Identification of critical Activities / documents from schedule point of view.
iv.
Interface scope document (discipline / activity wise)
v.
Pre shut down / shut down activities schedule.
vi.
Procurement schedule for the items in Contractor
’s scope of supply.
vii.
Project planning schedule highlighting the execution of project within the
Schedule.
viii.
Bio Data of persons involved in this project indicating their specific
specialization, scope of work and other requirements as listed elsewhere in the
tender.
Further drawing / documents shall be submitted in logical sequence / as agreed during
Kick of Meeting.
Page 27 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
10.8 All drawings / documents shall be thoroughly checked and duly signed and stamped by
Contractor, including drawings/ documents of sub-vendor, before submission.
Documents, which are unchecked, unsigned, and without revisions clearly marked, shall
be returned without review. Any delay on account of this shall be to the Contractor
’s
account. Drawings / documents submitted after incorporation of comments shall be
accompanied by
. Also, successive reviews of the same
documents shall apply only with respect to comments on the previous revision.
10.9 Documents once given Code-2 through mail shall be considered good for fabrication
along with compliance of the comments. Code-1 shall be considered as final approval
on the documents. Record category drawings will be provided comments for
incorporation with R category. Finally, As-built drawings, duly updated, shall be
submitted.
10.10 The successful vendor shall make fabrication drawing of the equipment and submit to
BPCL / EIL for review before commencement of fabrication. If any discrepancy is found
during fabrication stage, the fabricator shall inform BPCL / EIL immediately, and obtain
necessary approval before proceeding with relevant portion of the job any further. BPCL
/ EIL review of fabrication drawings and documents shall not be considered as a check
and shall not relieve the fabricator of his responsibilities to supply equipment as per
specifications. Fabricator shall remain responsible for discrepancies between his
drawings / documents and BPCL / EIL drawings / documents.
All drawings submitted by vendor to BPCL / EIL, shall be based on purchase document
and shall bear reference number and revision of the corresponding BPCL / EIL drawings.
In addition, it shall indicate item number, client’s name, fabricator’s name, purchase
order
number, drawing number etc., all in the lower right-hand corner. All revisions shall be
clearly marked by encircling with revision marks. Submission of required drawing /
documents shall be the responsibility of the vendor. Drawing index shall be prepared for
each item giving serial number, description of drawing, drawing number and revision
number. Updated index shall be forwarded along with each submission. Submission of
all required drawing / documents in time shall be responsibility of the fabricator.
Three sets of “As built” drawings and “Data folder” (containing test certificates, inspection
reports etc.), including one set of reproducible and electronic formats, shall be supplied
by the successful bidder along with the equipment. Soft copies of all drawings in
AutoCAD of version 2008 or later (.DWG format) shall be submitted to BPCL in DVD/Pen
drive as part of final documentation. Vendor shall ensure that all data folder documents
including the “AS BUILT DRAWING” are updated & complete
in all Design
Engineering respects and shall incorporate all suggestions/correction informed by BPCL
or the authorized inspector while obtaining certificate of completeness from the
Inspection Agency.
a.
All “As Built” documents shall be submitted in electronic format for storage in an
electronic document management system (EDMS) at BPCL-
MR
b.
A soft copy of the index of As Built documents in excel format giving document
number, document title shall be submitted along with final documents
c.
Multiple Documents shall not be clubbed as a single file.
d.
If documents are scanned, minimum scan resolution shall be 300 dpi.
e.
Document number shall be unique. Maximum file size for a single document shall
be 2MB. The file name shall be unique and shall not contain special characters.
Page 28 of 29
Format No. EIL 1641-1924 Rev. 1
Copyright EIL
All rights reserved
In the event of the fabricator’s failure to meet the above requirements, the supply of
equipment shall be considered as incomplete.
10.11 DRAWINGS/DOCUMENTS FOR SUBMISSION
All the deliverables (drawings/sketches) by Contractor, shall be in Autocad
Drawing, documents as per VDR below (refer other discipline VDR attached in
tender) shall be submitted through e-mail.
Considering revamp job, across the table reviews of the documents can be planned
by contractor for prompt Owner / EIL approval.
Text part of all the write-ups, etc shall be typed in MS-Word and Tables in MS-
Excel.
All documents for review, shall be provided in Electronic form
All As-built drawings / documents shall be provided in electronic form also besides
hard copies in requisite number as specified.
10.12 Contractor shall furnish the drawings/documents/data as per respective the VDR
mentioned below:-
Qualification requirement & brief execution
philosophy
With bid
Detailed Execution philosophy covering complete
pre shut down and shut down activities with
a) Dismantling activities of identified existing items
including dismantling drawing.
b) Erection activities (Including pressure part &
bought out items)
c)Modification Procedure / sequence for revamp of
heaters system with micro level planning for pre shut
down / shut down activities
d) Interface document for each activity / circuit to
identify all possible consumables / items
e) feasibility check of the layout for any fouling with
existing equipment / structure / cables etc.
X

planned for submission

side transfer line modification)
